title: QUIC CRT post-handshake ticket-bearing TLS update seam verification
preconditions: The repository builds successfully on net10.0.
The current client TLS, server client-auth, transfer, retry, and narrow CRT guard sets remain green.
REQ-QUIC-CRT-0128 remains a narrow internal ticket-bearing update seam rather than a claimed 0-RTT or resumption implementation.

supplemental_sections.heading: Acceptance Criteria
supplemental_sections.content:
- The managed client/runtime path can surface an opaque ticket-bearing update after Finished.
- Handshake completion and 1-RTT readiness remain unchanged by the ticket seam.
- The slice does not claim ticket persistence, replay ownership, PSK derivation, 0-RTT packet support, or any public API widening.

status_summary: This verification artifact records the landed internal post-Finished ticket-bearing update seam and points at the requirement-home and runtime evidence in the working tree. The real client-side OneRtt wire-ingress follow-on remains traced separately under VER-QUIC-CRT-0027.
scope: Verify an opaque post-handshake ticket-bearing update after Finished without claiming any resumption or 0-RTT packet path.
procedure: Inspect the transcript-progress, bridge-driver, bridge-state, and runtime seams for opaque post-Finished ticket surfacing through the internal update seam.
Verify that handshake completion and 1-RTT readiness remain unchanged when the internal ticket seam is exercised.

Run the focused REQ_QUIC_CRT_0128 requirement-home tests for post-Finished ticket surfacing, pre-Finished rejection, duplicate retention, server-role rejection, and unsupported OneRtt post-handshake message ignore behavior on the narrow internal seam.
Run the current CRT/API/INT guard set and the full `REQ_QUIC_CRT_` sweep.
Run repo-local SpecTrace validation and markdown render checks after the canonical JSON updates.
expected_result:
- The managed client/runtime path surfaces opaque post-handshake ticket bytes after Finished and stores them only as internal state through the narrow update seam from REQ-QUIC-CRT-0128.
- Unsupported OneRtt post-handshake TLS messages are ignored at this seam and do not broaden the implementation into a general post-handshake engine.
- Existing handshake, client TLS, server client-auth, transfer, and retry floors remain unchanged, and the public surface still does not expose ticket or early-data promises.
